title: Deferred merge queue & landing substrate — cross-repo ordering, self-approved PRs, ParallelChange lock release
summary: Prep research for decision #2138 (should lane landing move out of the producing run into a deferred merge queue drained by a unified command; report we:reports/2026-07-02-deferred-merge-queue-substrate.md; extends #1996's pr-flow-rollout-mechanism). IN-REPO GROUNDING: the #1933 clone/lane/push machinery + central integrator (merge-one-at-a-time, full gate per merge #1937, rebase-retry, impl-repos-first/WE-last order) are built and proven (16/18 landed 2026-07-01), living in-run in we:.claude/skills/batch-backlog-items/parallel-execute.workflow.js; the merge-risk lock layer is we:scripts/readiness/file-locks.mjs (15-min lease TTL) + the RESERVED_MERGE_RISK denylist in we:scripts/readiness/lane-partition.mjs; the active->resolved flip rides the WE lane commit (lands only on merge). PRIOR ART (web-verified): (1) GitHub's NATIVE merge queue is strictly PER-REPOSITORY — no cross-repo primitive (docs.github.com managing-a-merge-queue); it groups PRs onto gh-readonly-queue/{base} temp branches and gates the merged tree on required checks; author self-merge at 0 required reviewers is fully supported (the only block, 'author can't approve own PR', is moot at 0). (2) The Not-Rocket-Science-Rule / speculative-merge invariant — gate on the MERGED tree not the branch tip — is the settled correctness floor (graydon2.dreamwidth.org/1597, bors-ng, Zuul gating); WE already codifies it (#gate-on-merged-tree-lane-fast-fail). (3) CROSS-REPO ATOMIC LANDING IS SOLVED OFF-THE-SHELF — Aviator ChangeSets group PRs across multiple repos all-or-nothing, Zuul Depends-On serializes a cross-repo DAG in fixed order — but only as external SaaS/CI, NOT via gh; so 'cross-repo needs custom sequencing' is correct ONLY given WE's GitHub-native constraint (adopting Aviator/Zuul is a real, lock-in-rejected alternative). (4) ParallelChange/expand-contract (martinfowler.com/bliki/ParallelChange.html) confirms an ADDITIVE (backward-compatible) hunk can land AHEAD of its consumers while a rename/signature change cannot — so extracting the additive part of a shared file as an early out-of-order micro-merge that releases the lock early is a textbook expand-phase move. (5) Decoupling 'ready' from 'merged' via a separately-drained queue/train is established (GitLab merge trains, bors batches); the novelty here is only the manual drain command + cross-repo order. SYNTHESIS — 5 forks prepared: Fork 1 relation to #1996 'fully automatic' = classification/statute-reconciliation, default COMPATIBLE-REFINEMENT (automatic governs the merge DECISION — gate is sole authority, no human review/manual merge — not the cadence; a human launching the drain is a trigger not a review gate; add a reconciling rider); Fork 2 durable lane metadata = code-shape fork, default per-item MANIFEST committed in the WE lane commit (ref-name convention can't carry structured cross-repo order + blockedBy edges); Fork 3 merge-risk lock lifetime under deferred landing = merit fork, default FLIPPED BY THE SKEPTIC from raw-(c) to (a) SERIAL-REPLAY PRIMARY + (c) EXPAND/CONTRACT only as an opt-in optimization over a WHITELIST of provably-safe additive regions (append-npm-script-key / new-per-entry-registration-line) — a general line-diff additive-classifier is REFUTED (line-additive-but-semantically-breaking false-positives: dependency bumps, overrides, side-effectful registrations, on exactly the ③ files it targets; the micro-slice gates green in isolation and lands, breakage surfaces at a sibling's merge, so (c) degrades to (a) after a wasted round-trip); (c) must be codified as an amendment to #merge-risk-optimistic-with-targeted-lock (its ③-stays-locked rule) not only a #pr-flow rider; sub-fork A split-author = the DRAIN over the whitelist; sub-fork B DEMOTED by the two-confusion screen to a correctness obligation (verbatim cherry-pick → byte-identical no-op double-apply, gate is the net not the proof). Fork 4 real ready-to-merge state = representation fork, default AMENDED from pure remote-ref derivation to a LOCAL queued token written at push (claims.json-adjacent, read offline) + single ref-deletion after the whole couple's resolve is confirmed reachable — pure ls-remote derivation is rejected (TOCTOU re-offer window during cross-repo drain + a network round-trip on the ownership path contradicts Rule #105 'claim ignores git state, stays local'). Fork 5 merge substrate = merit fork, default FLIPPED BY THE SKEPTIC from the native-queue hybrid to PRs-AS-REVIEW/CI-SURFACE + the CUSTOM DRAIN owning EVERY merge in impl-first/WE-last couple-order with GitHub's NATIVE QUEUE OFF — the native-queue hybrid is REFUTED (GitHub's native merge queue is a BRANCH-LEVEL setting on WE main, so enabling it for solo WE lanes also grabs couples' WE-half PRs out of impl-first/WE-last order, and it splits the gate into two non-identical environments CI-vs-local); pure local git merge (a) is the coherent fallback (user direction 2026-07-02 pointed at PRs; #2151/#2152/#2153 build the PR arm). Layer: agent-workflow / session-tooling — nothing crosses the WE<->FUI standard boundary; codifies as a rider under we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md#pr-flow-rollout-mechanism.
